title: Tiered vault model
outcome: Tiered vault model documented and made machine-readable. .chezmoidata/vaults.toml declares exactly two non-overlapping vaults: pass (concern=interactive: CLI logins/API keys/script env, GnuPG, files=false) and sops (concern=files: values in git-checked config, age backend, files=true); both agent_readable=false. SECRETS.md is the companion doc (tiered split + ASCII diagram + rules: one vault per concern, sops encrypts values/keeps keys readable, no raw secret in any public config path, agent hard-block pointer). validate.sh phase-1 whitelists SECRETS.md as control/meta.
validation: validate-secrets.sh [1/5] PASS: 2 distinct concerns across 2 vaults (no overlap), all vaults agent_readable=false. validate.sh PASS (7/7) regression after SECRETS.md whitelist.
decisions: Vault contract lives in the dotfiles source (.chezmoidata/vaults.toml + SECRETS.md), mirroring E01's routing.toml + ROUTING.md, so S3 templates can consume .vaults and the validator can assert non-overlap by construction
Non-overlap is enforced by the validator (distinct `concern` values == vault count), not just asserted in prose
token_note: loaded 1 story block + 1 canon ref + STATE/ledger tail; no full-canon read
